name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 25.08.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 25.08.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As  on  22  August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1073.98 lakh ha as compared to 1038.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 97.9 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As  on  22  August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1073.98 lakh ha as compared to 1038.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 97.9 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be below normal in most of the country except some parts in South Karnataka.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be below normal in most of the country except some parts in South Karnataka.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01 -Aug 24 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 4% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)17%; (ii) Northwest India: 15%; (iii) Central India: 9%; and South Peninsula: 6%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01 -Aug 24 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 4% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)17%; (ii) Northwest India: 15%; (iii) Central India: 9%; and South Peninsula: 6%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy to very heavy rainfall  very  likely  to  continue  over  Gujarat  till  30th  and  over Rajasthan till 26th August with isolated extremely heavy rainfall also likely over East Rajasthan on 24 th  August, 2025.
texts.text: Heavy to very heavy rainfall  very  likely  to  continue  over  Gujarat  till  30th  and  over Rajasthan till 26th August with isolated extremely heavy rainfall also likely over East Rajasthan on 24 th  August, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated very heavy rainfall likely over Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana & Chandigarh on 24th; Uttarakhand during 24 th -25 th  August, 2025.  Increase in rainfall activity likely along the west coast from 26th August, 2025.
texts.text: Isolated very heavy rainfall likely over Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana & Chandigarh on 24th; Uttarakhand during 24 th -25 th  August, 2025.  Increase in rainfall activity likely along the west coast from 26th August, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Scattered  to  Fairly  widespread  rainfall  likely  over  northern,  western  India,  western peninsular and eastern India.
texts.text: Scattered  to  Fairly  widespread  rainfall  likely  over  northern,  western  India,  western peninsular and eastern India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over the most parts of the country.
texts.text: Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over the most parts of the country.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  21 st   August  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 142.01 billion cubic meter, which is 78% of total live storage capacity (182.496 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 107.6 percent of last year's storage and 119.6 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  21 st   August  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 142.01 billion cubic meter, which is 78% of total live storage capacity (182.496 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 107.6 percent of last year's storage and 119.6 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: text
texts.orig: water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Rice (Varieties: PR 114, PR 126 & PR 131) (Southern Rice Black Streaked  Dwarf  Virus  (SRBSDV))  in  Haryana,  (Swarming  Caterpillar  and  Leaf  folder)  in Assam,  (Yellow  stem  borer,  Leaf  folder,  Bacterial  leaf  blight,  Brown  plant  hopper)  in Chhattisgarh & (Armyworm (Ear Cut Worm)) in Meghalaya, for Maize (Downey Mildew) in Karnataka, for  Cotton  (Pink  ball  worm,  Angular  leaf  spot/Bacterial  leaf  blight)  in  Haryana and for Green Gram (White Grub) in Rajasthan. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Rice (Varieties: PR 114, PR 126 & PR 131) (Southern Rice Black Streaked  Dwarf  Virus  (SRBSDV))  in  Haryana,  (Swarming  Caterpillar  and  Leaf  folder)  in Assam,  (Yellow  stem  borer,  Leaf  folder,  Bacterial  leaf  blight,  Brown  plant  hopper)  in Chhattisgarh & (Armyworm (Ear Cut Worm)) in Meghalaya, for Maize (Downey Mildew) in Karnataka, for  Cotton  (Pink  ball  worm,  Angular  leaf  spot/Bacterial  leaf  blight)  in  Haryana and for Green Gram (White Grub) in Rajasthan. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (14 Aug - 20 Aug 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 20%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  20 th Aug 2025) is higher than LPA by 2%.
texts.text: During the week (14 Aug - 20 Aug 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 20%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  20 th Aug 2025) is higher than LPA by 2%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The monsoon trough at mean sea level now passes through Bikaner, Jaipur, centre of lowpressure area over central parts of south Uttar Pradesh & adjoining north Madhya Pradesh, Prayagraj,  Daltonganj,  Bankura,  Digha  and  thence  southeastwards  to  northeast  Bay  of Bengal.
texts.text: The monsoon trough at mean sea level now passes through Bikaner, Jaipur, centre of lowpressure area over central parts of south Uttar Pradesh & adjoining north Madhya Pradesh, Prayagraj,  Daltonganj,  Bankura,  Digha  and  thence  southeastwards  to  northeast  Bay  of Bengal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be above normal over most parts of northwest & central India;  normal  to  above  normal  over  many  parts  of  east  &  northeast;  below  normal  over many parts of south Peninsular India during the week.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be above normal over most parts of northwest & central India;  normal  to  above  normal  over  many  parts  of  east  &  northeast;  below  normal  over many parts of south Peninsular India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 22 August 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major parts of M.P, eastern parts of Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  major  parts  of  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Kerala,  Karnataka,  Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P., Tamil Nadu and southern parts of Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 22 August 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major parts of M.P, eastern parts of Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  major  parts  of  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Kerala,  Karnataka,  Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P., Tamil Nadu and southern parts of Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in northern parts of Bihar, in parts of Assam, in parts  of  Saurashtra  region  of  Gujarat,  in  pockets  of  Rajasthan  and  in  southern  parts  of Odisha.
texts.text: It was lower than average of past 9 years in northern parts of Bihar, in parts of Assam, in parts  of  Saurashtra  region  of  Gujarat,  in  pockets  of  Rajasthan  and  in  southern  parts  of Odisha.
